content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

August 21, 2006

Russell H. Miller
Attorney at Law
20 Park Road, Suite E
Burlingame, California 94010-4443

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-06-150

Dear Mr. Miller:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the campaign
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTION

Is the Committee to Recycle Bay Meadows, a primarily formed ballot measure
committee, required to file quarterly reports under Government Code section 84202.3(a)
until termination?

CONCLUSION

Yes, the committee must file quarterly reports under Government Code section
84202.3(a) until termination.

FACTS

You are the treasurer of a recipient committee, Committee to Recycle Bay
Meadows, FPPC ID# 1281550. This committee was primarily formed to oppose the
qualification of a referendum to overturn the City of San Mateo's decision to recycle the
Bay Meadows racetrack in San Mateo. If the referendum did qualify for the ballot, your
committee would have supported the decision of the city council to recycle the site. Your
committee has been filing quarterly campaign statements pursuant to Government Code
section 84202.3.

The proponents of the referendum effort were advised by the San Mateo County
Elections Department in December 2005 that their petition had insufficient signatures to
qualify for the ballot. In January 2006, the proponents sued the County Elections

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. A-06-150
Page No. 2


Department to have the insufficient petition signatures validated. On July 17, 2006, the
San Mateo County Superior Court rejected the proponents claim and sided with the
Elections Department that the petitions had insufficient signatures. As a result, the issue
will not appear on the ballot.

ANALYSIS

In addition to semi-annual campaign statements required by Government Code
section 84200, ballot measure committees are required to file quarterly statements during
each six-month period in which the measure is not being voted upon. Quarterly
statements must be filed on or before April 30, covering the period January 1 through
March 31, and on or before October 31, covering the period July 1 through September 30.
The quarterly reports are not required during any six-month period in which the measure
is being voted upon. (Section 84202.3.) In addition, the quarterly reports are not
required following the election at which the measure is voted upon unless the committee
makes contributions or expenditures to support or oppose the qualification or passage of
another measure. (Section 84202.3(c).)

Section 82043 of the Act defines a "measure" as any constitutional amendment or
other proposition that is submitted to a popular vote at an election by action of a
legislative body, or that is submitted or is intended to be submitted to a popular vote at an
election by initiative, referendum or recall procedure whether or not it qualifies for the
ballot. Although, no election was scheduled for the issue, the purpose of the committee is
to oppose a potential measure at an election. Section 84202.3 provides only two
exceptions for reporting on a quarterly basis: during any six-month period in which the
measure is being voted upon; or following the election. Until an election is held, or until
the committee is terminated, it must file quarterly campaign statements.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

John W. Wallace
Acting General Counsel

[signature]

By: Trish Mayer
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division

QUESTION: Is the Committee to Recycle Bay Meadows, a primarily formed ballot measure
committee, required to file quarterly reports under Government Code section 8a202.3(a)
until termination?

CONCLUSION: Yes, the committee must file quarterly reports under Government Code section
8 a202.3 (a) until termination.
